title: X Bench (Works with X Outdoor Table)
summary: A backless X-leg bench sized to pair with the matching X outdoor table. All 30° cuts — every leg piece is angled.
description:
A simple backless bench with X-shaped legs, sized to slide under a matching X-leg outdoor table. It's a clean, sturdy design, and the whole thing is defined by one angle: 30°. Every leg piece is cut at 30° off square, some with parallel ends and some not, and building the X is a matter of layering those pieces so the crossing arms nest.

Because it's meant for outdoors, build it from a rot-resistant species — cedar or pressure-treated — and use exterior screws and waterproof glue. The joinery is light (1-1/4" fasteners and glue for the legs, 2-1/2" screws for the top), which is fine because the seat sits in compression on the legs.

Build each X leg by attaching the two long leg pieces first, using the smaller pieces to guide the angle, then filling in the shorter pieces to complete the X. Cap each leg with the angled top blocks, then land the 2x4/2x6 seat boards across the two legs. Leave a small gap between the seat boards so rain drains through — an outdoor bench that pools water rots and stays wet after every shower.

tools.note: Every leg piece is a 30° cut — a miter saw makes them repeatable.

cutList.note: Both ends 30° off square, ends parallel.
Long point to long point; both ends 30° off square, NOT parallel.
Long point to long point; both ends 30° off square, NOT parallel.
Long point to long point; both ends 30° off square, NOT parallel.

steps.title: Start each X leg
steps.body: Use the smaller leg pieces to guide the angles. Attach the two longer leg pieces of each X with 1-1/4" fasteners and waterproof glue. Getting these two set at the right angle establishes the X.

steps.title: Fill in the X
steps.body: Fill in the spaces with the smaller leg pieces, using 1-1/4" fasteners and glue, to complete each X leg.

steps.title: Cap with top blocks
steps.body: Attach the angled top blocks to the top of each X leg with 2-1/2" screws and glue. These give the seat boards a solid, level landing.

steps.title: Attach the seat
steps.body: Land the 2x4 and 2x6 seat boards across the two legs and attach with 2-1/2" screws and glue. Leave a small gap between the boards so rain drains through — a solid outdoor seat holds water and rots. Sand any sharp edges and let cedar silver or apply an exterior finish.

images.alt: Backless outdoor bench with X-shaped legs
